With work, the mystic cloth could surveil the Moon Garou.

Perhaps Rasmodel could infiltrate them after all.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightHelmet:
It's hard not to look at the Black Knight armour and marvel. The Black Knights on Ashenfall have completely different origins from that of the mainland. They have no direct connection to the Kinshra, and are instead evolutions of the Shadow Knights. Two connected but ultimately different factions, yet they converge on similar armours. I suppose that's chaos for you. Stare at it long enough and you spot patterns.

The Black Knights, or Shadow Knights as they were originally known, began life as bodyguards for the Rasmodel family. The Rasmodels were nobility on the mainland, and retained that air of nobility in Ashenfall. They were invaluable to the Children of Shadows, as they could move in higher circles, and higher circles were where the seeds of chaos are best sown. 

The Children of Shadows made the mistake of seeing the Rasmodels as peacocks, preening and insufferable. As time passed, the Rasmodels exerted their influence over the Shadows. They moved into positions of leadership. Every pie had a Rasmodel finger in it, and suddenly the Children and the Rasmodels were synonymous. 

That influence came with demands. The Rasmodels wanted protection. Their scheming in Temple Woods and Bramblemead would lead to the odd fallout, and having bodyguards leant them legitimacy as well as protection. Some of the Children were assigned to the Rasmodel, and a different kind of seed was sown.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightPlatebody:
It's hard not to look at the Black Knight armour and marvel. The Black Knights on Ashenfall have completely different origins from that of the mainland. They have no direct connection to the Kinshra, and are instead evolutions of the Shadow Knights. Two connected but ultimately different factions, yet they converge on similar armours. I suppose that's chaos for you. Stare at it long enough and you spot patterns.

Very suddenly, the Rasmodels fell back to the walls of Dowdun Reach. Their cover was blown: the youngest had been caught pouring tincture into the ears of a sleeping bailiff. Their entourage and influence no longer meant anything, and they were forced to live in the keep. A wing was allocated to them, mostly out of fear: the bodyguards were loyal to the Rasmodels, and the Children of Shadows wanted to wake up in the morning.

On the death of the father, the eldest Rasmodel son, Milliburn,  looked to regain some of the family's lost pride. He burrowed himself deep into Dowdun: he was integral to the training of forces; he took charge of the quartermasters. He showed all the hallmarks of a leader, and was duly elected as Master of Dowdun Reach. The Children of Shadows took to calling him 'Lord Rasmodel'. His bodyguards never left his side.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightPlatelegs:
It's hard not to look at the Black Knight armour and marvel. The Black Knights on Ashenfall have completely different origins from that of the mainland. They have no direct connection to the Kinshra, and are instead evolutions of the Shadow Knights. Two connected but ultimately different factions, yet they converge on similar armours. I suppose that's chaos for you. Stare at it long enough and you spot patterns.

The Children of Shadows learned to be wary of Lord Rasmodel in the mornings. He was cruel before noon, fair in the afternoons, and stern all day. Those who were close to him knew the reasoning: Lord Rasmodel slept poorly, and would complain that his dreams kept him up. He was confused by them: they talked in words that he didn't recognise. He found their voices to be dull and interminable, like being locked in a room with a droning aristocrat. 

The dreams made him impatient and quick to bore. His relationship with Alric, Archmage, deteriorated. Rasmodel even suspected that Alric was behind his restless nights. 

One morning, Rasmodel emerged with a different kind of impatience. He was resolved that something had to change. Fellhollow, Imaru and Xikotal were not to be trusted. A new order of knights were to be created, and he was to lead them. At their centre would be his bodyguards, loyal and unquestioning. 

The Shadow Knights were born, and time would harden them into the Black Knights.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightCowl:
The Shadow Knights, or Black Knights, as they would come to be known, were mostly melee and ranged fighters. When magic was needed, they would enlist Alric's magi, but that secondment was always short. The melee knights were mostly posted in the grounds, but the ranged knights had a different role: they were stationed around the demon gate.

Lord Rasmodel gave his Black Knights two sets of orders. The first they were free to discuss, and were encouraged to do so. They were there to protect the magi from the demons. Should one break from its bindings, it was to be put down. In an outbreak situation, the Shadow Knights had full jurisdiction and a freedom to kill with impunity.

The second set of orders was not to be discussed, not even in the company of another. There were two salient instructions. 

Instruction 1: The Shadow Knights would allow for the 'Demon's Share'. Should a demon become unruly, one single mage fatality was encouraged before the demon was to be put down. It was like the culling of rabbits. The mage numbers needed to be managed. 

Instruction 2: Should the flow of demons be too much for the magi to control, the Shadow Knights were to collapse the infernal portal. This was the primary directive: the demons and magi were secondary.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightBody:
The Shadow Knights, or Black Knights, as they would come to be known, were mostly melee and ranged fighters. When magic was needed, they would enlist Alric's magi, but that secondment was always short. The melee knights were mostly posted in the grounds, but the ranged knights had a different role: they were stationed around the demon gate.

Archmage Alric came to Lord Rasmodel with a sheet of paper. It was the morning, when the Master of Dowdun was most impatient. 

Alric had been monitoring the Black Knights. He had suspected something for years now, but today he had proof. The sheet was from a ledger, and it accounted for the number of magi casualties since Alric had become Archmage. There were one-hundred and seventeen deaths, and they came from one-hundred and seventeen incidents. There was no single incident where more than one mage died. 

Alric was in a fury, tossing papers and spitting curses. It was proof that the Shadow Knights were deliberately negligent, that they saw the magi as disposable. Lord Rasmodel kept silent, and let the mage rampage.

The orders went out from Lord Rasmodel the next day. All Shadow Knights were to allow two magi to die with each incident.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightChaps:
The Shadow Knights, or Black Knights, as they would come to be known, were mostly melee and ranged fighters. When magic was needed, they would enlist Alric's magi, but that secondment was always short. The melee knights were mostly posted in the grounds, but the ranged knights had a different role: they were stationed around the demon gate.

Lord Rasmodel's greatest challenge came from within. It did not carry a sword or crossbow. 

The Shadow Knight numbers were dropping. It was impossible to resign from the order - that was written in the charter - but more and more of the knights were falling into madness. If it were physical wounds, Lord Rasmodel could have done something about the epidemic. He could amputate the problem with a single swing of his sword. But his enemy was intangible. 

He decided to visit those on the precipice of madness. Most were unintelligible, but some talked of food not satisfying them anymore. Others called out to the Dreaming Stone like it was their mother, as if it would save them. In many cases, it sounded like the afflicted were arguing with themselves: two enemies unleashing an enmity. 

One of the mindless disquieted Lord Rasmodel more than the others. She was an afflicted soldier that he knew from long ago. She rambled in a voice that mimicked the droning of a bored aristocrat. Lord Rasmodel knew that voice well. He stumbled from the bedside of the soldier and took no other visitors that day.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakMageHood:
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

The downfall was sudden. They would not admit it, but the magi had lost control of their demons. Their reaction was the same as always: to deny and deflect. If you accused the magi of summoning demons outside of sanctioned areas, they would smirk and give a carousel of different answers: you are lying; you are mistaken; you are cowardly; you are to be eaten by demons. 

Something terrible was bound to happen; it was a question of when. An infernal demon materialised during a Shadow Knight training session. The recruits were memorising guard rotations, so only their teacher was armed. It was a massacre. 

Alric and Lord Rasmodel locked themselves in the war room to argue. None were to enter. Only the two of them know what was said - oh, to be a raven, watching - but everyone knows the result. 

The Dreaming Stone, and the allocation of power that came from it, would be managed solely by Lord Rasmodel. The demon portal would have its power rationed.

Alric and his magi had their wings clipped.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakMageRobes:
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

At first, the changes to Dowdun Reach were positive. By throttling the power from the Dreaming Stone, there were fewer incidents at the demon portal. Demons rarely appeared without being summoned, and those summonings were restricted to the portal room. If the Reach was once a lightning rod for demons, it was now grounded with only the occasional spark.

Alric was never going to accept this new status quo. His faithful began re-routing the power of the Dreaming Stone. Energy tethers from the Dreaming Stone passed through and round residences. The Library, which warded against magical overflows, had its power siphoned. 

Where the Dreaming Stone's energy-flows were once arterial, they were now like capillaries. The energy spread everywhere, a webbing throughout the Reach. And that had consequences. 

Some of those consequences were explosive, sudden. The capillaries would overload, leading to fires and outbreaks. The magi would blame the Shadow Knights for these discharges, since Lord Rasmodel was now steward of the Dreaming Stone. 

Other consequences were less sudden. They barely manifested at all, but their effects were insidious.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakMageRobeLegs:
It's hard to look at what the Zamorakian Mages have become. They were once a noble order, committed to chaos, demonology and destruction. You would fear to be in their presence, particularly that of their leader, Alric. Now, you fear them for wholly different reasons. The Reach has twisted them into something bestial. 

Before the re-routing of the Dreaming Stone's energy-flows, people weren't exposed to the energy tethers for long. But now that Dowdun Reach was a lattice of Dreaming Stone energy, that changed. The flows ran through people's rooms as they slept. It pulsed and hummed and bled.

People began to change. Dreams became rarities; nightmares the commonplace. People heard whispers in quiet moments. Through lack of both sleep and solace, the magi were frayed at the edges. They would be quick to anger.

Then the infestation came. People fell sick; a great wave moved from the magi wing outwards. The lucky were feverish or comatose, left to die on stretchers. The unlucky carried battles in their heads. Voices warred within them, and they would buckle over in pain as screaming headaches took them. 

The epidemic was so terrible, so consuming, that Lord Rasmodel's actions might be considered understandable. He was desperate, and likely ill himself. 

Lord Rasmodel shattered the Dreaming Stone with his warhammer. It smashed - its shards tossed to the winds. For one glorious moment, the voices stopped. Lord Rasmodel felt peace in that moment. But something worse was unleashed, and the horrors doubled.
KeysToEntries.BlackShield:
More and more of the afflicted began talking in the voice that Rasmodel recognised: the voice from his recurring dreams. They were a chorus, moved from the land of dreams to his waking nightmare. Doubt burned in him. Was this epidemic his fault? Was it here for him? 

He couldn't hide his relationship to the epidemic anymore. When he walked past the afflicted, they would move from incoherent to coherent. They would sit up and speak to him. The message was the same: "submit to the Dreaming Stone". There were variations on that theme: use the Dreaming Stone; spread its energy; offer your mind to it.

Lod Rasmodel was resolved. He would never submit. Perhaps therein was the trap. Perhaps it was shrewd psychology to make him destroy the stone. 

But destroy it he did. All it took was a single blow from his warhammer. A great and savage arc. As the crystal shattered and the shards punctured his skin, he felt peace. The voices were silenced. he knelt, bleeding and happy. 

And then the aftershocks came.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakCape:
Zamorak never came to Dowdun Reach. Its gates were never graced by the mahjarrat. No general came, or ambassador from the mainland. Even the ZMI failed to send an offshoot to the keep. 

How was that possible? Chaos taught them that, by simple laws of averages, a high-ranking Zamorakian should have stumbled onto their shores. Ashenfall and Dowdun Reach was too much of an opportunity. Greed must have led a Zamorakian highborn here. 

The silence was deafening. Everyone who lived or worked in the keep knew what this meant: Zamorak had barred his inner circle from visiting Ashenfall, likely at pain of death.  How else could Dowdun Reach have been left alone for so long?

But why? Why did Zamorak keep Ashenfall at arm's length? The answers differed, depending on the zeal of the follower. Zamorak feared something on Ashenfall. Zamorak knew that something made it worthless. Zamorak was already here, just without Dowdun's knowing. Zamorak is in all of us.

Remarkably, that mystery only strengthened the beliefs of Dowdun's Zamorakians. By being so contemptible to Zamorak, they were utterly important. They were main characters in Zamorak's performance. 

It suggests another reason why Zamorak might have left Ashenfall alone. Perhaps he wanted to show that nothing - not even his disdain - would soften his people's infatuation.
KeysToEntries.DowdunReachCape:
The Dowdun Reach Cape is an emblem of your complete and utter mastery of Dowdun Reach. Congratulations.

You've seen things people wouldn't believe. You've attacked lesser blue dragons while on fire on the battlements. You've watched the Dreaming Stone glitter in the darkness near Dowdun Reach gate. All of these moments will get washed out, in time, like a wet cape in rain.
KeysToEntries.AdventureCape1DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.
KeysToEntries.AdventureCape2DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.
KeysToEntries.AdventureCape3DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.
KeysToEntries.AdventureCape4DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.

KeysToEntries.AdventureCape7DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.

KeysToEntries.AdventureCape10DowdunReach: A wise purchase. While it doesn't make you invisible or grant you super-powers, this cape does make a delightful swishing noise as you sashay.
KeysToEntries.StandardArtisanPotion:
While it might seem like a good idea to drink several artisan potions at once, the results aren't - unfortunately - multiplicative. The quality of what you make doesn't improve.

In fact, the effect is a bit like dancing when you're drunk: your confidence improves to the point that you THINK you're god's gift to crafting. But then the potion wears off and you see the results: you've made half a tunic with a pom-pom taped to the sleeve. 

Please drink your artisan potions in moderation, people.
KeysToEntries.StandardAttackPotion:
Vannaka doesn't believe in attack potions. Oh, he's aware that they might exist, and he has even given them to adventurers on occasion, but he doesn't want them to exist so doesn't believe in them. 

Vannaka, to almost no-one's surprise, is a combat purist. Battle is between two warriors with two blades. The dance is unfettered by things like potions, buffs and perks. If he had it his way, it would be unfettered by armour or clothes. Potions are just one more way that 'combat has gone to the dogs' and 'killing someone isn't as good as it used to be'.
KeysToEntries.StandardConstructionPotion: Alchemy is a magnificent thing. With innocuous ingredients like ground sandstone and irit, you can make a concoction that is focused on something as singular as construction. One rock and one herb can make you better at making an architrave or lintel.
KeysToEntries.StandardCookingPotion:
Ironically, a cooking potion tastes horrible. Truly, truly awful. Cooking Guild students have been known to stick two fingers down the throat to eject the potion. It's not worth it before an exam. 

Great chefs like The Culinaromancer stand by the potion, however. The belief is that it strips away the tastebuds so that new tastebuds can grow. Like coppicing a tree, the newer tastebuds are more refined.
KeysToEntries.StandardMiningPotion:
When a dwarf looks at a mineral deposit, they see all of the weaknesses in the rock - the right places to strike. They can spot the indents and inflections that suggest ore is present. They know how long it will take to exhaust the deposit so that they can head down the pub.

The mining potion captures a lot of that knowledge. For fleeting seconds, you can feel what it is like to be a dwarf.
KeysToEntries.StandardRunecraftingPotion:
It's wonderful to see how far the world has come. Before the First Age, no-one had runes. The only way to wield magic was to become a god, and those opportunities weren't particularly common. 

But now? Not only can you mine essence and make runes, you can use potions to min-max the making of runes. People are swapping strategies for runecrafting runs. We've gone from no runes at all to experience-per-hour. 

It makes you wonder what's next. What is impossible now, but people of the future will consider commonplace? I'm going for toilets, but INSIDE the house.
KeysToEntries.StandardWoodcuttingPotion:
When your experience of deforestation isn't rising fast enough, consider drinking a woodcutting potion. Before long, you will have chopped all of those pesky air-producing eyesores. 

You might even get the sap-rage, a berserker-like state where you just can't get enough of felling trees. Sap and other tree innards will fly before your axe. You won't remember any of it; sap-rage is all consuming.
KeysToEntries.HealingPotion:
Hold a healing potion in your hands and doubt might enter the mind. Do you drink it? Do you apply it directly to the wound? Is it a suppository? Well, allow us to clarify: it is taken orally. 

A healing potion won't just address your egregious malady. It will help with minor nuisances, too. If you've got a spot on the inside of your nostril, it will tidy that up nicely. An armpit itch? Gone in seconds. Ingrowing toenail? Consider it done.
KeysToEntries.SuperLumberjackPotion:
Unfortunately, the lumberjack potion won't garb you in a checkered flannel shirt and a moustache. Apothecaries are still working on that one.

Instead, the lumberjack potion will fill you with energy and enthusiasm for woodcutting. You're a chopping machine.
KeysToEntries.SuperQuarrymasterPotion:
As with many physical activities, you can over-exert yourself when mining. Lactic acid builds up in the muscles, sweat clouds the vision, and an accidental pickaxe swing can lodge in the head. A mining potion addresses all of these shortcomings. Lactic acid and sweat forms slowly. The likelihood of accidents is reduced.

What it can't do is improve the quality or amount of ore. Pour it in your mouth or pour it on the deposit: it won't matter. No mining potion is going to help you there.
KeysToEntries.AntifirePotion:
While the antifire potion is a miraculous thing - one of the most useful items on Ashenfall - it does have a flaw. It can be hard to know when its effect wears off. Knowing that you are protected, or not protected, while standing in front of a dragon is something of a problem.

One solution is to bring someone with you. Get them to count while you fight a dragon. Then they can give you a happy thumbs-up while protected and a sad thumbs-down when you're not. 

Of course, they probably need antifire if they're going to assume that role. Which means a third-party to give a thumbs-up or thumbs-down for that second person. Yeah, we see the flaw in the plan.
KeysToEntries.SauteedShrimp:
In a burst of freakish coincidence, the shrimp is both the easiest sea creature to catch, and the easiest to cook. As a result, some folk from seafaring towns have been known to call shrimp 'eggs of the sea'. Both are considered over-easy.

If you burned rather than sauteed a shrimp, congratulations! In some towns, a burnt shrimp is considered a rarity, such is the negligence required to make one.
KeysToEntries.FriedSardine:
A fried sardine, also known as a charred pilchard or 'pilcharred', is the go-to snack for a fisherman's pocket. If you see a stoic fisherman who talks very little, it's likely because they've got a mouthful of fried sardine and don't want to reveal the bones between their teeth.

Fried sardines were also the subject of a failed business venture by a dwarf called Drorkar. He tried to manufacture 'tins' that would contain these sardines, with smithed 'rings' to open their tiny metal coffins. It was roundly rejected and cost the dwarf a great deal.
KeysToEntries.GrilledTrout:
Until recently, grilled trout was prescribed by doctors thanks to its regenerative properties. 'A grilled trout a day will keep the gout away' was the common phrase. After a study by the Cook's Guild in far-off Varrock, however, it was found that regular eaters of grilled trout were 20% more likely to contract gout.

Don't let that stop you enjoying the bitter notes of a trout. The Cook's Guild did a study and found that 75% of gout-inflicted people thought it was totally worth it.
KeysToEntries.SearedAnchovies:
Not be confused with Seered Anchovies: a delicacy from Umbral Sands where the anchovies are lightly toasted on clairvoyants.

The naturally salty taste of an anchovy has been ascribed to salt flats on the ocean floor, where anchovies feed on the underwater flora. In an attempt to deepen the anchovy flavour, fishermen have been known to sprinkle pepper, marrentil and pineapple on those same salt flats.
KeysToEntries.SmokedHerring:
Smoked herring is not, as the idiom will claim, 'red'. It is more of an orange, coppery colour. That means the term 'red herring' is something of a red herring. Which tracks.

The process of smoking a herring has changed over the years. In the Second Age, a herring would be smoked in the village elder's pipe, in the hope that some of the elder's wisdom would transfer to the fish. In the Third Age, entrepreneurial Zamorakians would line their demon portals with herring in the hope of an infernal aftertaste. Nowadays, people use a cooking pot.
KeysToEntries.FriedSalmon:
Fried salmon may not be the most substantial or healing of fish, but it's celebrated for its moist texture and delicious taste.

Famously, it was the subject of a smear campaign from the Saradominist church to get it banned as a foodstuff, as they objected to the populace describing it as 'moist'. They deemed the adjective 'unsaintly'.
KeysToEntries.RoastedLobster:
On the sea voyage to Ashenfall, Zanik went through a period of shouting 'lobster!' at the end of perfectly normal conversations with her shipmates. She claimed it was a joke that young dorgeshuun would play on each other, but it led to her being roundly ignored by Vannaka and Dionysius for several days. 

It's also a roasted food - one that makes you feel more refreshed and less thirsty after eating it. If you are to attempt to cook one, it is recommended that you... LOBSTER!
KeysToEntries.BraisedUndeadEel:
Sweet Saradomin on High, why on earth are you braising an undead eel? Are you a mind rune short of a rune pouch?

Perhaps it's a dare, and you're going to get a hefty payout after making it? I can't think of any other reason, sane or otherwise, why you would fish for the horrible, wriggly thing, pick it up afterwards, then braise it, which - we may add - is a process to DRAW OUT FLAVOURS. 

What next? Bury it in the ground for days and eat it afterwards? I would not put it past you.
KeysToEntries.GrilledUndeadBass:
Only moderately less disgusting than braising an undead eel is grilling an undead bass. At least you can close your eyes and imagine what the bass once was: a tasty fish that took a wrong turn at Ardougne and ended up in the Withering. 

At some point in the future, some forward-thinking chef will determine that grilled undead bass is a delicacy. Right now, however, we look at you with the stinkiest of eyes and think 'for shame'.
KeysToEntries.DriedKrill: Did you know that, for a short period in the First Age, time was once measured in Krill Hours? If watches existed back then, you would have been able to set it by the migratory patterns of krill. The dominant shoal would take precisely one hour to pass from one side of Bramblemead Valley, round Temple Woods, to the other side of Bramblemead.  Children would often try to match their speeds, attempting to see who could notch up the greatest KH (krill hours).
KeysToEntries.BraisedCatfish:
Cooking a braised catfish is no mean feat. The process involves removing the armoured shell while managing to avoid the nerve-endings that, once severed, can release a poison that is fatal to humans. Then a chef needs to achieve precisely the right temperature to neutralise the latent poison without - again - activating it. 

Of course, if you want fewer friends, braised catfish is easy as pie to cook.
KeysToEntries.SmokedInfernalEel:
One of the most challenging dishes to prepare on Ashenfall is the smoked infernal eel. The whole process is an olfactory gauntlet. You are bombarded with such a succession of smells, some wonderful, some horrifying, that it can feel like sprinting through the back alleys of Varrock. Some chefs from the Cook's Guild have compared it to 'seeing one's life flash before their eyes'. Except with noses.

Complete the gauntlet, however, and the bounty is worth it. Many people call smoked infernal eel a 'delicacy' but the term doesn't really suit. Delicacy is derived from 'delicate', after all. The taste is more like a salt-chinchompa exploding under your tongue.
KeysToEntries.ShrimpOmelette:
It was inevitable that shrimp, known as the 'eggs of the sea' for being over-easy to catch, would get combined with actual eggs to form a meal. Shrimp Omelette is the go-to feast for a shipman, often whipped up by the ship's cook and delivered en masse to the crew. If you're assigned to a long-haul crew, it's best to get a taste for the 'Shrompy' (as it's often shortened). 

Shrompy is also common among Wizards' Guild students who only have space in their dorms for a frying pan and some fire runes.
KeysToEntries.SauteedSardines:
Redberries are red

Sardines are grey

This is the meal 

I eat while I slay.
KeysToEntries.HerbBakedTrout: Cathan was known for his love of herb baked trout. He would often claim it was 'the best thing you could do with a tater'. That was until Vannaka picked up a potato, threw it halfway across Temple Woods, and knocked the Party Hat off of Dionysius's head. Cathan subsequently admitted that herb baked trout was 'the second-best thing you could do with a tater'.
KeysToEntries.EggsRoyale:
Do you know what they call an eggs royale in Asgarnia? 'Salmon and egg'. Barbarians.

There aren't many flavours that go together as well as salmon and egg. Pork and cheese. Bread and butter. Fishing potions and a hangover.
KeysToEntries.BerryDrizzlePanSeared:
"Pan-seared what?", you may ask. Well, pan-seared herring and anchovies, my good fellow. That might sound like a lot of fish in one meal, but some dwellberry acts as a sweet binding agent, making the whole eating-process rather delightful. It's a meal that's been around for generations, and you will often hear battle-worn adventurers wishing that they were back home for 'some of mammy's pan-seared'. 

We suspect that the berry drizzle pan-seared that you've made is NOT from mammy, however. We may be wrong: it's very possible that there's a quest that we've forgotten.
KeysToEntries.UndeadDelight:
'Undead Delight' sounds like an audio track from a long-forgotten Halloween event, but NO - it's an unexpectedly tasty meal. The very dead flavours of an undead bass are meshed with the very alive flavours of a tomato, and the combination is capital-D Delicious.

We will admit that it's off-putting that the un-alive also find the Undead Delight tasty. Put it in front of a skeleton, zombie, demon or other wretched being and they will gobble it up like there's no tomorrow.
KeysToEntries.FishFry:
A 'fish fry' is something of a misnomer as only the eel is a fish, and even that's questionable now that it's undead. The lobster is most definitely a crustacean, and a cabbage is a holy orb of sustenance, delivered by the majestic Brassica Prime. Therefore, not a fish.

We give a fish fry a pass, mainly because it's a Fellhollow delicacy, once favoured by Ravanna. It was invented after a sailor saw a lobster and eel fighting and threw a well-aimed cabbage. He fried the result and lo, fish fry was born.
KeysToEntries.StirFry:
Imagine if we named all our meals after the processes that make it. Ooh, I'm going to make a pot of boil-strain-pour. I might follow it up with a batch of roll-cut-bakes. It's all a bit odd, if you ask me. 

The Ashenfall stir-fry is something of a curio when compared to the mainland's. To make a mainland version, you would have to swap the krill for seaweed and the infernal eel for lava eel. Whisper it, though: I think the Ashenfall version is better. And it goes particularly well with some grow-chop-fries.
KeysToEntries.StuffedCatfish:
Perhaps it's a good thing that fish have no idea what their future holds. We can only imagine their reactions. "Sorry, chap, you're going to have your armour removed, your innards replaced with cabbage, and krill pushed into your orifices". 

Would it soften the blow if they knew how tasty they became? A stuffed catfish is elevated by the brassic flavours, achieving culinary peaks that are unrivalled on Ashenfall. We like to think that the knowledge would soften the blow.
KeysToEntries.WeakFocusedFishingPotion:
'Weak' and 'focused' may seem like an oxymoron, but you have to look at the terms from a different perspective. When we say 'focused' we mean you - the angler - and your concentration. It is a measure of how much you narrow your eyes, quell your breath, and attribute all of your senses to the timeless art of poking hooks into fish. This potion increases those faculties. You become so focused that nothing else in the world matters. 

And by 'weak' we mean that the potion is a little bit rubbish at doing all of the above.
KeysToEntries.LesserFocusedFishingPotion:
If you're one of those anglers who exclaim that fishing is 'about taking a load off' and 'more about relaxation than the end-product', then you might want to try a lesser focused fishing potion. It removes the need for excuses and offers a chance of actually catching fish. Your experience grows and you will soon be gathering catches on the regular.

As an added bonus, with a higher fishing level, you can look down on all the other anglers who claim they fish 'to take a load off'.
KeysToEntries.VeggieSandwich:
Brassica Prime approves of this sandwich.

Although, the mushrooms are a little much: they could probably have been thinned out. Maybe removed? Yes, remove them. The bread, too. Is it needed? Would you perhaps consider removing the envelope of bread that houses the sweet cabbage missive? Your dribbly jowls will thank us.
KeysToEntries.MeatSandwich: Hold up, hold up: are you sure you want to do this? You are about to cross the meat streams. Flank steak and rat roast: it's never been done before. Who knows how the two meat types might react between slices of bread.
KeysToEntries.MapleToastie:
Ah, the sweet gooey goodness of a maple toastie. On its own, the maple syrup might have been too much, but the dried redberry? It ties it together with a little red bow.

There's the added bonus that it's bursting with natural sugars. Now you can use those energies to go mine and woodcut some natural resources.
KeysToEntries.SteakSandwich:
Steak, if you ask me, is infinitely diminished by putting it between two slices of bread. Steak should stand proud on its own, garnished with salt and barely introduced to a flame.

As a journal-writer I can't stop you. My powers are limited to shaming you from the dark recesses of your interfaces.
KeysToEntries.DoubleMeatSandwich: The 'twist' in the meat twist is a delightful redberry jus. The 'twist' in the Anima Twist is not, unfortunately, a delightful redberry jus.
KeysToEntries.GlazedRoastPumpkinSandwich: Not only is the roast pumpkin sandwich 'glazed' because it is doused in delicious maple syrup; it is also 'glazed' in the modern parlance, since it is 'showered with excessive, unwarranted or exaggerated praise'. Which makes it double-glazed. Like a window.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightCrossbow:
The crossbow is the ranged preference of the Black Knight, mainly thanks to its stopping power. An avernic demon will rush a target at distance and then slash in close quarters. Having a crossbow stops a demon from getting to that troublesome second stage.

Our assumption is that you are not a demon, but the sentiment still applies. You will want to avoid a crossbow-wielding Black Knight at distance, and instead move in for a spot of slashing. You need to search for the demon inside yourself.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightMelee:
Insurance coverage as a Black Knight must be inordinately expensive. You'd need indemnities against demon attacks, dragonfire, magi and even your own leader, the Black Knight Titan. That's before you get into the intangible stuff: madness, demonic infestation, the Withering.

Imagine surviving that gauntlet of threats, only to see adventurers arriving on boats. Adventurers love killing knights: it's a genetic predisposition. 

Spare a thought for the Black Knight, weathered by life's challenges. Consider letting them live today. The Champions' Guild have even cooked up an initiative: GAKAB month. It stands for 'Give a Knight a Break' month.
KeysToEntries.Alric:
Ask the demons about Alric and among the goading, lies and chaos there will be a pattern. The demons believe that Alric has signed a pact with a foremost demon on Infernus. The contents of that pact are unknown, but it's fun to guess.

Alric has been alive for many centuries. That immortality could be tied to a demon pact. His abilities in the realm of demonology are also unparalleled: he can summon them at a rate that would make Bilrach weep. It makes sense that a pact would amplify this ability. 

There are far-fetched guesses that seem more unlikely. One posits that the 'foremost demon' is, in fact, the god Zamorak. Demonic translation is an imperfect art, after all. That pact has kept Zamorak away from Ashenfall, so that Alric can claim it as his own. 

And the last is that Alric asked to be a demon himself. The immortality, the affinity to demons, the burgeoning power: all of them could be attributed to a centuries-long transformation. Even if it's untrue, it's a fascinating thought.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightHoundmaster:
Among all the ranks and roles of the Black Knights, the one that is most sought-after is the houndmaster. It is not difficult to see why: life as a Black Knight is lonely and life-expectancy is low. Having a four-legged friend can make life an increment brighter, particularly when the hellhound is its own source of light.

It's also notable that, among all the enmities that exist in Dowdun Reach, there is none bigger than the one between hounds and ravens. The ravens taunt the hounds, and the hounds would dearly love some raven between their teeth.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightTitan:
The Dreaming Stone had been shattered. 

Lord Rasmodel, on his knees, heard no voices. For the first time in too, too long, he could hear his own breath. His heart played a welcome beat. For decades, he had felt like he floated above the world. Now he was part of it again.

Footsteps. Lord Rasmodel lifted his head wearily. Who would interrupt this moment?

Ringing around him was his family. The Rasmodels. They were all there - all the ones he knew, loved and had met. They were dressed as the finest nobility, the Rasmodels at their height. They had regained their standing - the name meant something again. 

His father knelt down and lifted Lord Rasmodel's bloody chin. Their eyes met. "Rest", his father said. "We will take it from here."

Milliburn Rasmodel, twenty-fifth Lord of Rasmodel, took his retirement.
KeysToEntries.LesserBlueDragon:
Lacrussa, the creator of the lesser blue dragons, saw life in a different way to you or I. While we might approach a new creature with wariness or curiousity, Lacrussa would approach it with an intellectual hunger. The creature would be mentally dissected for its physical suitability, it's cognitive capacity, or its magical resonance. She would turn it in her mind, this way and that, determining how it might factor into her experiments. 

Then came dissections of a more physical nature. She would cut and dice and drain. She was an expert in biological reaction, forcing revulsion and attraction out of living matter. She would take the results of those combinations and cycle them through the same experiments. Cut and dice and drain. 

Knowing that experimentation begat experimentation, it's hard to pinpoint the materials that gave us the lesser blue dragon. If there is one certainty, it is that Lacrussa herself is part of them.

There are indications that, around the time that the blue dragons moved to Dowdun, Lacrussa was experimenting with lightning. It is possible that Lacrussa found a means to bottle it in a lesser blue dragon.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightGuardian:
Chaos is an attractive philosophy until you're the one organising guard and patrol rotations. Chaos gains a little asterisk: you need a little organisation to facilitate the chaos. 

Those on guard rotations know that threats are just as likely to come from within than without. For this reason, veterans would advise the newer knights to glue a mirror to the back of their shield.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightWarrior: Lord Rasmodel rarely entrusted the rookies with any meaningful work. He found that they arrived with chaos in their hearts: they weren't old enough to realise that chaos is an ideal and rarely works in the day-to-day. So, he would give them the role of 'Warriors', and would loosely allocate them an area of the Reach to wander. "Let their cynicism grow", Rasmodel would say. "If the demons or magi don't kill them first, they might harden into one of the good ones."
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightBrute:
Being a Black Knight Brute wasn't just an acknowledgment of fortitude. Lord Rasmodel used them for tasks where conscience was a burden. 

They would be called upon for public executions, where a message needed to be sent. They were always on-call for demon outbreaks. Latterly, they would deal with extreme outbreaks of the madness. It was all too common for the voice-infected to move together in a chorus, a large group whose aim was to kill indiscriminately. Brutes would quickly put a stop to them.
KeysToEntries.SergeantBlister:
You wanna know why they call me Blister? Course you do. You'd do well to listen up.

People love to gripe and fight in 'ere. Lotsa madness, lotsa grievances - it's gonna 'appen. But they know not to fight wiv'out Blister. I get called tout suite.

I'm the one who handles the bets. You wanna make some extra dirt from a fight? You come to me. I give fair odds.

Why Blister? I'm the one who grows fat an' happy from all the friction in this place. Like a blister on your foot, ain't it?
KeysToEntries.HoundmasterRexiton:
Houndmaster Rexiton doesn't realise the power that he wields. If he wanted to, he could trigger a huge power-shift in Dowdun Reach. The hellhounds obey his voice. His key, and his key only, turns in their locks each night. With a pointed word or a neglectful thought, he could cause chaos.

Rexiton would never turn on the Reach because, at heart, he is a devout romantic. It's a combination that Lord Rasmodel noticed quickly and capitalised on.

Rexiton was devout to both Zamorak and the Reach. He was an idealist who believed that Dowdun could become the centre of Gielinor. With Zamorak in the ascendance, and Dowdun so heavily fortified, he earnestly believed it could become the capital of the known world.

Rexiton also wanted nothing more than for everyone to get along. The rifts of Dowdun Reach were seen as temporary to him. If he could form a bond with a hellhound, then the world could drop its squabbles and come together in peace.

Lord Rasmodel couldn't think of a more lovable idiot to be in charge of one of the Reach's most formidable weapons.
KeysToEntries.BlackKnightSniper:
My Black Knight Snipers. While others look inwards, you are to look outwards. Your patrol is the horizon; your quarry is dragons.

Now that Fellhollow's guardians are dead, the orders have become simpler. Lesser blue dragons are to be spared; the balance is to be preserved. But any other draconic presence - they are to be shot on sight. 

You are the foremost marksmen and women of the Reach, so we expect you to find your targets. Fire at the dragon's head, chest and belly. When it looses fire, aim for the mouth. Follow these commands and glory will be yours.

Death to all dragons.
KeysToEntries.MageOfZamorak:
You have three masters. The first is Zamorak. The carpet we lay for him is red and frilled with chaos. 

The second is to me. As your archmage, my word is pact. Every conversation we have is signed with your soul. 

The third master is Dowdun Reach. It vibrates with our power. It fills with our works. Should anyone compromises it, friend or foe, I give you leave to destroy them.
KeysToEntries.SummonerFlament:
Summoner Flament's experiments were sanctioned by Alric but given a wide berth by everyone else. He was less interested in the comings and goings of demons - how to bring them into this world, how to shuffle them out - and was more concerned with demonic behaviours. He wanted to see what the average avernic demon would do in a myriad of situations: what do they do when starved? How do they behave when in the company of other, non-avernic demons? Could they drown? Could they be dropped from a great height?

Summoner Flament had a pit. It was in that pit that he would keep his testing demons. He would get an allocation from the magi (who would shuffle away before they had to interact with Flament). Alric knew that Flament's works were unorthodox but produced results that could be leveraged against the demons.

And it was from the top of the pit that Flament scratched notes, watched, and sang.

"Ten red demons, sitting in a pit. Ten red demons, sitting in a pit. And if one Black Knight should accidentally slip...

There'll be ten red demons, chewing human bits."

There was madness in Summoner Flament before the Dreaming Stone shattered.
KeysToEntries.RatMasterSkrexis:
There is no 'Rat Master' position in the Black Knights or Zamorakian Magi. 'Rat Master' is very-much self-appointed.

Rats would come and go through the banquet hall, the dorms and the library, and Skrexis would watch them. There was one rat - fat as a carnivorous chinchompa - that led them. One eye was scarred, and its claws were long, made for gouging. Skrexis monitored the rats' movement, noting when they would be in each room. The rats had a timetable; they were predictable.

Skrexis waited for the fat, leader rat in the kitchens. He sat, cross-legged, with a saucepan in his lap. A wave of rats poured into the kitchen, saw Skrexis and flooded back out. But the fat rat, it approached, one eye locked on the seated man.

Skrexis wished there was someone, a bard perhaps, to have seen that fight. It was for the ages. The two combatants scratched, lurched, swung and skittered, but Skrexis emerged victorious. Bloodied, he approached the rats who had been watching from the doorframe.

Skrexis had earned the title of Rat Master, by way of combat.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakianAcolyte:
Are you sure you want to be an acolyte? It's long hours, thankless, and the Staff of Subjugation will be constantly whipping your shins. People don't usually volunteer for the position. 

You'd be doing yourself a favour. Even the life-expectancy of a demon summoners is longer than an acolyte. You might even make it into your thirties.
KeysToEntries.LesserDemon:
It's not difficult to find out what the Black Knights think of demons. You only need to visit the banquet hall or dorms. Any Black Knight will tell you that lesser demons are feckless, cruel and abhorrent to most senses. Make a Black Knight lord for a day and their first commandment would be to seal up the demon portal.

But understanding what the lesser demons think of the Black Knights, or other humans in Dowdun Reach? That's a tougher task. What we do know is that demons will eat a human if they can get away with it, so 'foodstuff' will factor in. They are quick to trap the gullible into demon pacts, so 'easy mark' is another. And they resent being pulled into this world against their will. So, 'hateful'.

Hateful, easy-mark foodstuffs. Doesn't paint an attractive picture, does it?
KeysToEntries.Delrom:
This is not Delrom's first summoning rodeo. He was summoned once before into ScapeRune - a twisted, upside-down version of RuneScape. 

To his dismay, he was the subject of a quest called 'Demon Lover'. Let's just say that the only demon rings were the ones that adventurers wanted to put on his finger.

In the end, he managed to unsummon himself by wielding Darklight and saying the magical phrase Reba Melrac Muhtniremac Odnibag Iahcrup. It was a close call.
KeysToEntries.Thammarith:
Thammarith has been to this festering plane before. He fought in the God Wars on the side of a chaotician called Zamorak.

He was no Tsutsaroth, so he could not command a position as a lieutenant or general. He never saw this 'Zamorak', whom he fought for. Rank and file was very much his position.

What he did see, though, was a sight that lingered. In a cave that glittered with ice, a winged woman flew. She cut through his fellow demons with speed, slicing before they could throw up claws to protect themselves. He caught glimpses of her: stern looks and flashes of red, but he must have carried those glimpses with him for centuries.

When he found himself back in Gielinor, summoned through an infernal gate, he surprised himself. His first thought was of that woman, and whether he might see her again. No matter what he did in the Reach, the thought could not be shaken. What had she done to him?
KeysToEntries.Hellhound: Hellhounds are not dogs, nor are they from hell. Impious Beasts would be more appropriate, but it doesn't have the flow or the alliteration. They also couldn't care less what you call them: they're more interested in your souls.
KeysToEntries.LoyalHellhound:
'Loyal' hellhound implies that the other hellhounds aren't, in some way, loyal. Which is utterly correct: the other hellhounds are as loyal as a dagger to the back. Given half a chance, they would play fetch with your larger intestines.

But Houndmaster Rexiton knows their secret. They are impossibly vain. If you tell them that they are good boys - such very good boys - then you can get them to do anything.
KeysToEntries.Gnasher: Of the two sisters, Fido and Gnasher, Gnasher is the menace. She will come crawling out from a magma swim and then shake the lava out. She gets the 'zoomies', crashing through bookcases in the library. Sometimes, she dreams about eviscerating foes in her sleep, gouging clumps out of the wall with her twitchy feet. She is, as Houndmaster Rexiton puts it, a little scamp.
KeysToEntries.Fido:
Fido is the quiet, thoughtful one. When Gnasher shakes herself off after a lava-swim and accidentally immolates a Black Knight, she watches, licking her lips. She encourages Gnasher to enjoy some zoomies, pointing her at Zamorakian magi and bookcases full of explosive tomes. As Gnasher twitches in a dream, Fido is whispering in her ear.

You have to watch out for Fido. She is biding her time for something.
KeysToEntries.Hellrat: Hellrats like to be addressed in CAPITAL LETTERS AT ALL TIMES. As the lowest forms of demon, it makes them feel important.
KeysToEntries.Curly: Curly resents being called curly. Her hair merely bends back towards her body because she is SO HELLISHLY ALLURING.
KeysToEntries.Boggs: Boggs would like to sit on your head and pull your hair so that you make HELL-RATATOUILLE.
KeysToEntries.Reek: Reek doesn't reek. He just has an aroma THAT IS UNAPPRECIATED IN HIS OWN TIME.
KeysToEntries.Nosey: Nosey watched you while you were sleeping. She thought you were TERRIBLE AT IT.
KeysToEntries.Pip: Pip is allergic to apples, which makes her name DEVIOUSLY IRONIC.
KeysToEntries.Patch: Patch likes evil stew but would prefer if you ordered it for him as he is UNAPOLOGETICALLY INTROVERTED.
KeysToEntries.Raven: The raven wakes early and perches on the battlements of Dowdun Reach. There it can watch for adventurers. It longs for them to suffer a grisly end, not because a raven feels malice, but because a grisly corpse is easier to feast on. The food has been taken out of its wrapper.
KeysToEntries.CarnivorousChinchompa: Nature can often miss a punchline. Carnivorous chinchompas do not, in fact, like to chomp on chins. They aim a smidge lower and prefer jugulars.
KeysToEntries.CursedEssence:
Cursed essence is a magical agent often used in tanning. It is also something of a curiosity, one that a simple tanner can't readily explain. It goes further than simply binding itself to material; it latches onto it, almost parasitically. 

Demonologists have a theory that, although cursed essence drops from avernic demons of Dowdun Reach, it is actually cthonic in nature. It would explain the tendril-like tenacity.
KeysToEntries.MithrilDeposit:
Doric is peeved that mithril is predominantly in Zamorakian territory. Dwarves feel a kinship to the ore: it means a little more to them than the other metals. In his view, Zamorakians don't deserve it.

He won't admit it if you confront him, but it's a big reason that you can find him in Dowdun Reach. One eye on the mithril. If he had it his way, he'd be laying minecart tracks all the way from Dowdun Reach to Temple Woods. Then he'd have to fight the urge to make mithril platebodies until the end of his days.
KeysToEntries.LimestoneRock:
If you like the look of this rock, imagine what it could like in your house. Carve off a few chunks with your pickaxe and save up for a fetching limestone interior. The white will complement your eyes.

You might be surprised to learn that Vannaka refuses to use limestone in any capacity. Someone told him that you can contract lime's disease from it, a rash that pains the joints and nerves. "Can't be spoiling these reflexes", he'd say, swinging an axe.

Don't try to correct the man - it's not worth it.
KeysToEntries.MapleLogs:
Maple is a versatile tree. It's a formidable material, making some of the finest fishing rods, staves and bows. Its sap is also a glorious thing, capable of being made into nature's nectar: maple syrup.

Its leaf is also smooth and large enough that it can be folded over numerous times, which makes it useful if you're caught short in the forest. Which, we should add, is not, and will not be, a consideration for you. It is extremely unlikely that a 'being caught short', hunger-like bar will be added for you to maintain. Realism can be pushed too far.
KeysToEntries.MithrilOre:
Mithril means much to the dwarves. It was first discovered by a dwarf called Tolkun in the First Age, and its seams have been uncovered in much of Gielinor. The dwarves call it 'The Dwarven Welcome' thanks to its shine. Find the ore when mining and it lights up to greet you: a dwarven welcome. 

A 'Dwarven Welcome' is also what dwarves call a headbutt while wearing a mithril helmet.
KeysToEntries.Limestone:
Turn your limestone this way and that and you might come across a fossil. Lesser blue dragons have been in the region for some time, and there were experimentations that led to them. Being experimentations, they weren't cognitively blessed, so it's entirely possible to find the talon of a dragon that got trapped in a rockfall.

You could also find the odd chicken wing from the Second Age. The Children of Shadows loved a shake and bake.
KeysToEntries.BlueDragonhide:
Blacksmiths hate this one simple trick. They spend their lives mastering interlocking chains and plates, only for dragons to be born with them. A third of the blacksmith's punters are now gone, hunting for dragons rather than dropping some coin at their forges. It's enough to make a blacksmith sob into their anvil. 

Blue dragons hate that one simple trick too, of course. Their venn diagram overlaps with the blacksmith's: they'd rather people got their armours from somewhere other than dragons.
KeysToEntries.MysticFibre:
The origin of mystic fibres, thread and cloth are wreathed in mystery. There's little to no mention in any written histories, which - on its own - seems unlikely. It is not a subtle material. There should be log after log of merchants trading it, or actively avoiding trading it. 

It leads me to believe that the mystic material is an elaborate troll. It's a plant - and I don't mean that in the floral definition. I think someone has introduced it as a joke, and I have a list of potential culprits. Brassica Prime: you are number one on that list.
KeysToEntries.DreamShard: A little like a conch, you can put this infernal shard to your ear. But instead of hearing the sea, you might hear the mumblings of an ancient mind. It's no less soothing, though. If you ever have trouble sleeping, we recommend the calming white-noise of a fractured malevolent soul.
KeysToEntries.VileAshes:
Demons do not leave bones behind on death. That would require a skeleton, a prospect that would make a demon queasy. Instead, demons are magical entities, composed of essence and energies. On death, they disperse, leaving behind an ashy composite.

Don't be mistaken in thinking that it's similar to the ash left after a fire. Dab your tongue in vile ash and you'll soon understand where the 'vile' comes from.
KeysToEntries.BlackMetalBar:
Congratulations on killing enough Black Knights to form a bar from their resulting scraps. Don't think about the children you have orphaned, the dreams that you have impaled on the sharp end of a greatsword. Don't think about the concentrated misery you hold in your hands. 

It is rather shiny, though, is it not? Black Metal, particularly if you have the draconic sort (more chromatic, less matte), is mesmerising.
KeysToEntries.BlueDragonBlood:
What, you were expecting blue dragon blood to be blue? You wouldn't expect human blood to be flesh-coloured, would you? Why does that though disgust me so much?

Blue dragon blood is an apothecary's dream. It's volatile, and therefore heavily reactive, but it has a kind of built-in warning system. It will change colour and emit a sulphurous stench for a few seconds before reacting, giving ample time to evacuate.
KeysToEntries.MapleSap:
It's like the maple tree wants to be cut down. Not only does it produce hardy, versatile wood for tools and weaponry, it bleeds sap that is only a single step away from being maple syrup. If you want proof of the gods' existence in RuneScape, you need only look at maple trees. 

The maple tree gets its revenge in subtle ways. Its sap is inordinately sticky and too much syrup will give you a paunch. They're small victories, but the maple tree takes them.
KeysToEntries.BlueDragonScale:
There is a curious trend on Gielinor, whereby the most fragile fighters - archers and magic users - are the easiest to spot. From blue dragonhide to mystic robes, the fashion is to wear garish colours as if the rules of the animal kingdom apply. But no, archers and magic users aren't poisonous, venomous or hoping to be mistaken for a bee.

We wonder why that lack of subtlety might be. In the case of blue dragons and their scales, perhaps it's a cruel joke played by the dragonkin. They have seen that humans keep killing their creations, wearing their hides as trophies. They've got their revenge by way of a generous use of the rainbow.
KeysToEntries.MysticThread:
There are many steps to making mystic robes - fibres to thread, thread to cloth, cloth to robes - but you would be wise not to skip any of them. Habberdash the Impatient, a magician who lived in Temple Woods, attempted to make his robes solely from thread. He would wrap himself, round and round, with spool after spool of mystic thread, and then attempt battle.

You can imagine how it went. He got snagged on a goblin's helmet and quickly unwound, mid-battle. We're not sure who would have been more shocked: Habberdash, who promptly got stabbed in the gut, or the goblin, who was confronted with a wizard in his undergarments.
KeysToEntries.MysticCloth:
It is almost imperceptible, but mystic cloth hums. It's the magical resonance: the elemental threads pushing against each other as their charge shifts. That oscillation also means that there is a lightly massaging effect on the wearer. It's entirely possible to feel calm from the vibrations as you stride into battle.

You might also be mistaken for thinking that mystic cloth is blue. On a microscopic level, it's actually a multitude of colours, combining to form the illusion of blue. As a magician once told me, it's the same effect as a blue sky: it is the result of all the colours in the world combining to make wondrous azure.
KeysToEntries.MithrilBar:
In the Third Age on Ashenfall, mithril was incredibly desirable. It was only available in small quantities then, as its scarcity - perhaps inevitably - made it the fashion of the time. High-priests would incorporate it into their rosaries. Community leaders would wear it as brooches. It was rare enough that people would comment when they saw it. "Ooh, Rosie's doing well for herself". That sort of thing. 

The idea of a mithril bar would have been mind-boggling to anyone in the Third Age. No matter their alignment, they'd have stabbed you in the belly and run away cackling with it. But here you are with an entire ingot. Take a moment and consider yourself a Third Age king.
KeysToEntries.BlueDragonhideLeather:
A discerning archer would do well to craft blue dragonhide leather. The colour is fetching, the defence against magic is second-to-none, and the material has an elasticity that makes it perfect for the nimble stuff that archers tend to do.

If it has a downside, it's a little tight. There's no hiding in blue dragonhide chaps, for example: all the lumps and bumps are out there.
KeysToEntries.BlackMetalScraps:
Fun fact: black metal is actually steel that has gone through a form of vulcanisation. Demonic or draconic sulphur are used in the process, to slightly different effect. Demonic vulcanisation gives the metal a matte finish, while draconic is more chromatic. 

What you have here is some scraps of black metal. Could be demonic, could be draconic. They're likely off-cuts from the smithing or vulcanisation process, or it's entirely possible that they were bashed off a Black Knight's armour when you boffed them. Regardless, waste-not, want-not. Now you have the material required for some new gear.
KeysToEntries.CursedLeather:
You may wonder where the 'cursed' moniker comes from. There is no need to worry: you're not cursed by wearing it, and you're not going to be visited by the three dragons of past, present and future in the night for making it. It has a wildly different etymology. 

The 'cursed' is a tannery thing, you see. Cursed leather is notoriously hard to work and shape. Only the foremost tanners can manipulate it. But even they have a hard time, often exclaiming "cursed leather!" at the top of their lungs.
KeysToEntries.DemonicOil:
Combine corrupt bonemeal and vile ashes and the result is demonic oil. We should be specific about what kind of oil this is: please do no pour it into a frying pan or use it on a squeaky hinge. This is potent oil, as the 'demonic' prefix will attest. It is used for crafting, and solely for crafting.

Now you've got us wondering what would happen if you did actually add it to a frying pan or a squeaky hinge. Obviously, please don't do it. But if you do happen to do it, through an accident or momentary lack of wisdom, could you let us know what happens?
KeysToEntries.GroundLimestone:
Clearly, all limestone is ground limestone, as it comes from the ground. In many places it IS the ground. But this is specifically limestone that has been ground down into a dust. 

Ground limestone's primary use is in glassblowing. Should you have the crafting capability and a healthy, strong puff, you can use the ground limestone to make molten glass and then glass-produce.
KeysToEntries.GlassVessel:
The glass vessel. The emblem of potential. It could find itself filled with anything. Water, demonic oil, maple syrup. It could smash before it ever sees use. It could languish in someone's inventory, forgotten for months. In many ways, we are all glass vessels. 

Check back in your journal for other delightful moments of poignancy and philosophy. You may find your life unutterably changed by the wisdom there.
KeysToEntries.MapleSyrup:
We are yet to find something that maple syrup does not elevate. Pancakes and bacon are the obvious ones, but try it out on things that are more left-field. Rat meat? Elevated. Terrorbird meat? Elevated. Undead eel? Eelevated. 

So, thank you, humble maple tree. You are a true wonder of the culinary and apothecary world. It won't stop us woodcutting you, but know that each cut comes with a maple syrup-sized dollop of guilt.
KeysToEntries.BlueDragonDust:
Blue dragon dust is one of those things where you wonder who invented it, and what curious sequence of events led to it happening. Some unknown person had the wherewithal to strip a dragon of dragon scales, crush those scales in a pestle and mortar, then apply the resulting dust to a potion. Not only that, they downed said potion and stood in the path of a dragon. Does that not seem inexorably mad to you? 

But thank you, inexorably mad person, because now we have this potent ingredient which can protect a person from dragonfire. You have been forgotten in time, but your blue dusty legacy lives on.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakWarriorPack: A bag of personal effects carried by the warrior class of Dowdun Reach. Rummage around inside: you've earned it.
KeysToEntries.ZamorakMagePack: The knapsack of a mage within Dowdun Reach. It contains all the materials that the mage would have needed for a day's work. Now they are yours.
KeysToEntries.LesserAshes: Amid the sulphurous pile are some items. Don't be scared: reach a hand in and claim what's yours.
KeysToEntries.SmallAshes: The magnificent bounty of a demonic kill. Want to know what an infernal creature carries around with them on their day-to-day? Look no further!
KeysToEntries.MapleTree: A tree full of bounties, whether it's maple logs for crafting needs or maple syrup for cooking. The maple tree knows how much you need its wares and it's keeping a ledger. You are in debt to the maple tree, and the maple will come to collect.
KeysToEntries.CoarseNet:
Pity the poor shrimp or lobster, approaching the final moments of its life, only to find themselves in a coarse net. The snobbish among them might look down their antennae and think 'couldn't it have been a wool net, perhaps, or linen?'. 

We're inclined to agree with them. They have minutes before they get cooked in a pot, yet they don't receive the simple courtesy of a comfy net. When you're on your deathbed, we'll wrap you up in a coarse net and see how you like it.
KeysToEntries.WoolNet:
Wool is absolutely rubbish in water. Throw a sheep in a lake if you don't believe us. The water soaks into the wool and turns the poor animal into a sad, soggy anchor.

That rubbishness is - conversely - rather useful when it comes to nets. The wool clods up, leaving barely any holes for a shrimp to make a getaway. It's also heavy as sin. The marine life is thoroughly pinned. 

Now, getting the water out of the net: that's another matter entirely. You might do well to leave it out to dry in the Umbral Sands.
KeysToEntries.LinenNet:
Oooh, look at you, making a fine linen net. Lah-di-dah. 

I bet you're the kind to make a linen net and never use it. You just drape it on your side-table at dinner parties, so people can compliment it and ask where you fish. "Oh you know, here and there", you say, idly gesturing to a mounted bass on your wall (bought from a shop). But we see you, fisher-dandy. We see you very well.
KeysToEntries.MysticNet:
Brassica on a Bullock, that net is garish. You're not going to lose that, are you? I bet that your eyes lock onto it every time you open your inventory. First thing you see. Boom - MYSTIC NET.

It has the same effect on marine life, of course. They're merrily swimming about, doing whatever lobsters and shrimp do, and then boom - MYSTIC NET. Not much they can do, is there? They're transfixed, drawn in by the brightest of blue colours, like moths to a mirrorball. 

Which explains why the mystic net is rather formidable as a net. Not the most fashionable, mind.
KeysToEntries.AshRod:
Ah, the ash rod. It's been here from the First Age, and will be here for many Ages to come. Ever-reliable, and gets the job done. Line up all the tools in the world and the ash rod will likely be named the most productive and certainly the most loved. 

All hail rod ash!
KeysToEntries.OakRod:
Oak rod is much like its anagram: it's an A-OK rod. Not brilliant, not terrible.

It's a rod that you will you look back on fondly when you wield the mighty maple rod. "Ah, I remember when I used to use an oak rod", you will say, as you bite the head off a live catfish and stand astride a pile of infernal eels. 

For now, appreciate your time with it. You will miss it once you have left it behind.
KeysToEntries.WillowRod:
The weeping willow is a sallow tree, known for its love of water. Find a brook or ford and a willow will be there, lost in its reflection. 

That familiarity with water makes willow a strong wood for fishing rods. There's the merest wisp of magic running through it, an affinity for water. The fish recognise that affinity and relax. They circle until they're caught, which is pretty handy for an angler.
KeysToEntries.MapleRod:
This is it. You've made it now. The pinnacle of Dowdun Reach fishing: ownership of a maple rod. Don't let it go to your head. The hook will get stuck in your nostril. 

The humble angler doesn't draw attention to the maple rod. They don't wax it or buy it a nice kebbit-fur sheath. They don't name it something like 'Deborah', or kiss it before they go to bed.

No, the humble angler strides into the middle of other anglers and says "levels?". They listen to the fishing levels of others and nods with appreciation. 

Be the humble angler.
KeysToEntries.MithrilPickaxe:
A sign of mining distinction, the mithril pickaxe will get you noticed if you hang out with the right mining crowds. Doric in particularly will give you an appreciative wink. He might even toss you a rock cake or two. 

The mithril pickaxe is cold to the touch, incredibly reflective, and - most importantly - strong as an ox. You will no doubt have already realised that it makes short work of any deposit you find.
KeysToEntries.MithrilLoggingAxe:
It hurts Doric a little that you've used his prized mithril to make something that chops wood, but don't worry - he can be something of a snob. Dionysius often reminds him that he uses wood in his minecarts, pickaxe handles and toothpicks. 

Take some time to enjoy your increased capacity for woodcutting. There is a simple joy in watching your own increased efficiency.
KeysToEntries.MithrilDagger:
Doric likes to remind Vannaka that you wouldn't find a dwarf making daggers.

As Doric says, "it's a very human thing, a dagger. A dwarf never hides their weapon: we're polite enough to show what you're up against. An' you won't find us attacking from the back or sides. No sir, we bonk you from the front."
KeysToEntries.MithrilSword:
Mithril may not be the strongest metal on Gielinor, but it's perhaps the most beguiling. It's the metal that benefits most from looking at it up close. Each sword has unique imperfections.

It's a quality that makes it the most popular choice for ceremonial swords. Go back in time and watch a knighting ceremony in Dowdun Reach, or a bestowal of honours in Bramblemead. You will see your share of mithril swords.
KeysToEntries.MithrilGreatsword:
"You see a mithril greatsword, I see a mithril dagger," Vannaka would say. He would then pick two up from Doric's forge, one in each hand, and try not to register effort. It was a routine, a little play, between Vannaka and Doric. 

This time, however, the joke was on Vannaka. Doric had made his mam's rock cakes, and folded some mithril round them - one for each hilt. 

Vannaka wobbled. "Cripes," he said.
KeysToEntries.MithrilMace:
The standard term is mace, but Vannaka prefers bludgeon. "It's one of those sound words, like stomp and grunt, right? When I hit someone with it, it bludges. Bludge, bludge, bludge."

"You're thinking of onomatopoeia," Dionysius said, helpfully. 

"Nah, but good try. I'm thinking of breakfast," said Vannaka.
KeysToEntries.MithrilGreataxe:
In the early years of Dowdun Reach, the concept of a mithril greataxe would have been unthinkable. A big, showy weapon that used large quantities of mithril would have been seen as a betrayal. The Children of Shadows, holding a monopoly on mithril, wanted to present it as a rare metal. At best, the richest on Ashenfall might have a brooch or tiara that sparkled with mithril. 

Nowadays, Doric can have two greataxes whipped up for you before dinner. Much has changed.
KeysToEntries.MithrilScimitar:
The scimitar form is not commonplace on Ashenfall. Unlike the mainland (where you can't walk five metres in the Kharidian Desert without getting slashed by one), the scimitar is a rarity. Only an offshoot of the Garou, found in some of the harshest deserts of Ashenfall, are known to wield it. 

That is likely to change, of course, as more adventurers make landfall. It makes you wonder what the island will look like in five, ten years' time as the cultures intertwine.
KeysToEntries.MithrilWarhammer:
The mithril warhammer was once the weapon most associated with Lord Rasmodel of Dowdun Reach. He was rarely seen without it. In the mornings, when he was most in pain, it would be a prop, something to lean on while the night's watch recounted the events of the early hours. In the afternoons, it was a beautiful warning, a threat of what might happen if you disobeyed him.

Lord Rasmodel wasn't one for naming his weapons. He found the custom a little juvenile. But when his thoughts were elsewhere, you might see him pat the head of the warhammer. There was kinship there, even if he wouldn't admit it.
KeysToEntries.MithrilShield:
Doric insists that you should be able to see your face in a mithril shield. That's the mark of quality: if you can check for rock cakes in your teeth before battle, then you've made a good one. 

There's at least a little science behind that. The more tempered mithril is, the more it reflects. Doric isn't being a narcissist here: he knows what he is doing.
KeysToEntries.MapleStaff:
The Children of Shadows were never a grateful sort, but they would acknowledge that the groves of maple in Dowdun Reach were a boon. Almost everything wooden in the keep - from the library bookcases to the bannisters - was made from maple. 

Alric relished walking among the maple trees. For so much of his life, he had been encased in stone and surrounded by essence: working long hours in the keep. But the maples were for thinking. 

In his twilight years, Alric carried a maple staff with him: the Subjugation Staff. It was a crutch for an old man, yes, but it was also a way to bring the grove with him. When he couldn't muster the energy to visit his favoured trees, he carried a remnant with him.
KeysToEntries.MapleShortbow:
Putrilius, the cthonian demon, will tell you that any living thing can be possessed. If needs must, a cthonian can be a cockroach or spider, for a time. 

In one desperate moment, after the Dreaming Stone was shattered, Putrilius was forced to possess a maple tree. It wasn't an experience she enjoyed. Most curiously, she found themselves aligning to the maple's inner cadence. Everything slowed. Putrilius barely registered a thought in two years.

What woke Putrilius was a logging axe. Their maple body was toppled and then fletched into an armature. For a moment, as Putrilius's life faded from the wood, she was a shortbow. 

It was the closest she had come to dying. With a cognitive spark, she moved from the bow to a passing hound before all her lifeforce ebbed away.
KeysToEntries.MapleLongbow:
A supple wood, is the maple. When cut into long, reed-like lengths, it can be formed into a worthy longbow. The process also releases syrup - a treat for a day's fletching. Not enough to make a maple toastie, mind, but enough to leave a sweet taste in the mouth.

Bramblemead was often the venue for archery competitions, and the maple longbows would be out in force. Etched into the trophy, you would find many Rasmodels. They were a noble bloodline on Ashenfall, and would often add to their reputation by earning combat trophies.
KeysToEntries.MithrilCrossbow:
A mithril crossbow is the standard issue for any modern Black Knight. If you showed capability, then you might be promoted to Black Knight Sniper, and that came with the Black Sniper Crossbow. But for the average Black Knight, the crossbow was a long-kept friend. 

The crossbow's main purpose was to stop demons in their tracks. The Zamorakian magi claimed to keep their demon hordes under control, but demons tested that claim.

The crossbow had other uses. Remove the bolt, rest the crossbow on the floor stock-upwards, and you had a stool. Sit on the floor and it was a headrest. 

You could also waste time shooting ravens. Shooting the red, glowing ravens was an immediate dismissal, however. Alric demanded they were protected, for reasons that he never expanded on.
KeysToEntries.MithrilCrossbowLimbs:
What do you call a crossbow without limbs? A REALLY cross bow. 

Oh shush, you. That joke plays well at fletching conventions.
KeysToEntries.EnchantedMithrilCrossbowBolts: Goodness, you must really want to kill someone. As if punching through their armour with a crossbow bolt wasn't enough, you want to magically scupper them too. Remind me not to get on your bad side.
KeysToEntries.BlackSword:
Where the mithril crossbow is the standard issue of the ranged Black Knight, the black sword is standard for footsoldiers. 

'Black', you may be aware, is not a material. It is instead a descriptor for steel that has gone through a process similar to vulcanisation. There was a time when the vulcanisation was performed by the dragon Xikotal, as a gift to the isolationist knights.

With his death, the process moved towards the demonic. The resulting quality is lower, which has made the original, dragonfire 'black' metal sought after in Dowdun Reach.
KeysToEntries.BlackSniperCrossbow:
Climb the turrets of Dowdun Reach in its prime, and you would have found the Black Knight Snipers. These were the elite marksmen of the Black Knights, tasked with looking outward when the rest of the order would look inward. They would scan the skyline for threat - for the beating wings of a dragon. There was no greater trophy for the Black Snipers.

One one occasion, a bolt was loosed at Imaru. She had arrived at the keep for diplomatic purposes, and was rewarded with a bolt to the neck. You could see the conflict within her, as her urges were swallowed. For a second, a disdainful eye moved to the Black Sniper. But she reset and continued to talk to Lord Rasmodel and Alric like nothing happened.

Restraint was certainly beaten into the Black Snipers that night.
KeysToEntries.SubjugationStaff:
Anyone who worked with Alric knew about the Subjugation Staff. It would thud, thud, thud on the cobblestone floor as he approached. It was a welcome warning that Alric was arriving, but it came with notes of dread. 

The power of the Subjugation Staff came from what was rolled up and inserted into its middle. It was a demon pact, but one that had been defaulted on. A demon, not a human, had failed to keep their side of the bargain, and that gave the contract a radiating power. Alric captured that power within the staff. While he held it, it was as if all of demonkind owed him something.
KeysToEntries.UnholySymbol: For those who wish to keep Zamorak close to their heart, we give you the unholy symbol. To complete the look, we recommend a black top, eyeliner, sticking your tongue out and making Zamorak horns with your hands.
KeysToEntries.BrokenTitansSword: Take Lord Rasmodel's broken sword. It's a suitable remnant of him: robust and true to his beliefs, but shattered into pieces. You may even find means of remaking it.
KeysToEntries.TitansWrath:
Lord Rasmodel has been put to rest at last.
